name: Blood Test
meta_description: Learn about blood tests: purpose, types, preparation, and how to interpret results.
description: A blood test is a laboratory analysis of a blood sample to assess health status, diagnose conditions, or monitor treatments.
purpose: Blood tests can detect infections, nutrient deficiencies, organ function, cholesterol levels, blood sugar, and many other health markers.
procedure: A small blood sample is drawn from a vein, usually in the arm. The sample is sent to a laboratory for analysis.
preparation: Some blood tests require fasting for 8-12 hours. Your doctor will provide specific instructions.
normal_values: Normal values vary by test type and laboratory. Common tests include complete blood count (CBC), chemistry panel, and lipid profile.
abnormal_results: Abnormal results may indicate infection, anemia, diabetes, kidney or liver problems, or other conditions requiring further investigation.
cost_range: $50 - $500+ depending on the type and number of tests

name: EKG/ECG
meta_description: Learn about electrocardiograms: purpose, procedure, and what the results mean for heart health.
description: An electrocardiogram (EKG or ECG) is a test that records the electrical activity of the heart.
purpose: EKGs detect heart rhythm abnormalities, heart attacks, heart muscle damage, and other cardiac conditions.
procedure: Small electrodes are placed on the chest, arms, and legs. The machine records the heart's electrical activity over a few minutes.
preparation: No special preparation is needed. Wear comfortable clothing that can be easily removed.
normal_values: A normal EKG shows regular heart rhythm with specific wave patterns (P wave, QRS complex, T wave).
abnormal_results: Abnormal results may indicate arrhythmias, myocardial infarction, hypertrophy, or other heart conditions.

name: CT Scan
meta_description: Learn about computed tomography scans: purpose, procedure, and safety considerations.
description: A CT (computed tomography) scan uses X-rays to create detailed cross-sectional images of the body.
purpose: CT scans diagnose tumors, infections, injuries, and structural abnormalities in various parts of the body.
procedure: The patient lies on a table that moves through a large doughnut-shaped machine. The machine takes X-ray images from multiple angles.
preparation: May require fasting, contrast dye, or specific clothing instructions depending on the area being scanned.
normal_values: Normal results show no abnormal masses, lesions, or structural abnormalities.
abnormal_results: Abnormal results may show tumors, fractures, bleeding, infections, or other conditions.
